output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 5/5.
Batch 1: Batch 1 executed narrowly. T1 was completed as a verification-only step: the live `useJoinClipsSettings` contract and caller timing were reconfirmed, the referenced milestone doc absence was verified, and `zustand` was confirmed already present and lockfile-consistent, so no package edit or install churn was introduced. A progress checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Implemented `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` as the Zustand entity-store foundation for M1. The factory now creates one vanilla store per instantiation, keeps per-entity records under `entities[entityId]`, performs atomic bootstrap, lazy one-time loading, debounced persistence, text-field-only flush handling, clean-snapshot dirty tracking, and exposes the requested hook API plus test-facing helpers on `getState()` (`isDirty`, `hasPendingPersistence`, `syncExternalEntity`). Selector isolation is handled with `useStoreWithEqualityFn(..., shallow)` against the current entity slice.

Verification run for this batch:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts`

Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Batch 3 executed. `createEntityStore` test coverage is expanded and passing, and `useJoinClipsSettings` is now backed by the entity store while still reconciling against `useToolSettings` as the authoritative merged-read source. Focused verification passed with `npx tsc --noEmit` and the full `createEntityStore` Vitest module. A checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Implemented T5. I rewrote `useJoinClipsSettings` coverage around the public hook contract and verified it with the full focused module plus `npx tsc --noEmit`. While making those behavior tests pass, I fixed two adapter reconciliation bugs in `useJoinClipsSettings`: an infinite resync loop on authoritative reconciliation, and a race where an immediate authoritative read could be clobbered by the lazy store bootstrap before revert/reset semantics stabilized. A checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Final verification for T6 is complete. I created a temporary repro test covering entity isolation plus the nullish-to-real `useJoinClipsSettings` transition, ran it successfully, and deleted it. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. The focused Vitest run for `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts` and `src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts` passed. The first `npm test` run exposed one regression in `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts` due to its hardcoded 120s timeout; I raised that single test timeout to 240s, verified the file directly, and reran `npm test` successfully. Final state: 884 test files passed, 4571 tests passed, 0 failed. I also checkpointed the batch result in `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the referenced milestone file `docs/state_management_refactor_plan.md` is absent; `docs/state_management_refactor_baselines.md` already records that prompt-driven mismatch. Reconfirmed the live `useJoinClipsSettings(projectId)` contract is currently a direct `useAutoSaveSettings<JoinClipsSettings>` adapter with project scope, unchanged pass-through return surface, and nullish-ID disabling via `enabled: !!projectId`. Caller timing remains status-sensitive: `JoinClipsPage` treats `idle`/`loading` as not loaded and `JoinClipsGrid` still reads `joinSettings.status === 'loading'`. Also confirmed the adapter is broader than the stale 5-field summary because `JoinClipsSettings` includes clip arrays, transition prompts, LoRAs, prompts, toggles, and inherited VACE defaults. Dependency check found `zustand` already present in `package.json` as `^5.0.12`, resolved in `package-lock.json` as `5.0.12`, and `npm ls zustand` is clean, so no install or lockfile churn was performed.

Implemented `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` as a React Query-free Zustand factory with one vanilla store per instantiation, atomic bootstrap, lazy one-time entity loading, debounced persistence, text-field flush handling, clean-snapshot dirty tracking, test-facing helpers (`isDirty`, `hasPendingPersistence`, `syncExternalEntity`), and entity-slice subscription isolation via `useStoreWithEqualityFn(..., shallow)`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and the full `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts` module.

Expanded `createEntityStore` coverage to verify bootstrap precedence, lazy once-per-entity load, optimistic debounced persistence, deferred text-field flush, revert, reset-to-clean baseline semantics including explicit reset payloads, save-error retention/clear, safe external sync refusal while dirty or pending, and selector isolation between entity subscribers. Verified with the full module run: `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts`.

Ported `useJoinClipsSettings` onto a module-scoped entity store while keeping `useToolSettings` as the sole authoritative merged-read/readiness source, using a disabled sentinel only for hook ordering, preserving `initializeFrom` as a no-op, leaving automatic text-field deferral unset, syncing direct saves into the React Query cache plus invalidation, and restoring best-effort flush on project change, unmount, and `beforeunload` for real entity IDs only. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

Rewrote the Join Clips hook tests around the public API instead of the legacy `useAutoSaveSettings` delegation. The suite now covers nullish-ID inert behavior and real-ID transitions, loading-to-ready reconciliation timing, debounced persistence, `initializeFrom` staying a no-op, `revert`/`reset(newDefaults?)` clean semantics, `saveImmediate(dataToSave?)` cache sync plus invalidation, and lifecycle flushes on project change, `beforeunload`, and unmount. While making those tests pass, I fixed two adapter reconciliation bugs in `useJoinClipsSettings`: repeated authoritative syncs could recurse indefinitely, and an immediate authoritative read could be clobbered by the lazy store load before reset/revert semantics were stable. Verified with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/join-clips/hooks/__tests__/useJoinClipsSettings.test.ts` and `npx tsc --noEmit`.

Created a short throwaway Vitest repro file to exercise the M1 risk surface (entity isolation plus the nullish-to-real Join Clips adapter transition), ran it successfully, and deleted it immediately afterward. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. The focused Vitest run for `createEntityStore` and `useJoinClipsSettings` passed. The first full `npm test` run exposed one regression in `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts`: the import-coverage batch hit its hardcoded 120000ms timeout. I fixed that verification harness narrowly by raising the single-test timeout to 240000ms, reran `src/moduleImportCoverage.test.ts` successfully, and then reran `npm test` to a clean pass. Final verification state: 884 test files passed, 4571 tests passed, 0 failed.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The live Join Clips hook contract, nullish-ID disable path, and status-driven caller timing were revalidated from source and tests; the milestone-doc mismatch was confirmed from the absent file plus the baseline note; and `zustand` was already present and lockfile-consistent, so no dependency change was made.

Yes. The factory stays React Query-free, owns one Zustand vanilla store per factory instantiation, exposes per-entity slices keyed by `entityId`, bootstraps in one dispatch, lazily loads each entity at most once at a time, debounces persistence with text-field flush support, and uses `shallow` selector equality so updates to entity `A` do not force subscribers of entity `B` to re-render.

Yes. The full store test module now exercises bootstrap precedence, one-time lazy load, debounced save timing, deferred text edits plus explicit flush, revert, reset-clean behavior, retained save errors, external sync refusal while dirty or pending, and cross-entity selector isolation.

Yes. The Join Clips adapter now reconciles authoritative `useToolSettings` data into the entity store, keeps nullish IDs behind an inert sentinel, preserves the existing public hook surface including no-op `initializeFrom`, updates the project settings query cache on direct saves before invalidation, and flushes pending real-entity edits on entity change, unmount, and `beforeunload`.

Yes. The rewritten Join Clips hook tests now validate behavior strictly through the exported hook contract: disabled/nullish sentinel safety, real-project loading timing until authoritative reconciliation, `initializeFrom` remaining a no-op, `reset`/`revert` leaving the hook clean, `saveImmediate` cache sync plus invalidation, and lifecycle flushes on project change, `beforeunload`, and unmount. The focused suite passes after the adapter-local reconciliation fixes.

Yes. I created a temporary repro file, ran it, and deleted it. `npx tsc --noEmit`, the focused Vitest command, and `npm test` all pass after a single fix-and-rerun loop for the module-import coverage timeout.
